summary: Implemented the governed project starter standard. The new specbridge-project-starter command creates deterministic local starter artifacts under .specbridge/project-starters, records project identity, MVP scope, non-goals, blocked scope, future SpecBridge specs, agent architecture, validation plan, security prompts, and strict safety boundaries before any real implementation or external mutation. Claude Code delegation was attempted but did not complete due provider timeout/availability, so ChatGPT/Codex completed the task inside the active contract.
validations: GitHub Actions SpecBridge Review Gate: passed
GitHub Actions SpecBridge PR Review Report: passed
GitHub Actions Claude Review Non Blocking: passed
policy_result: Within policy. The command is local and deterministic, writes only to the declared .specbridge/project-starters/<safe-task-id>.project-starter.json path when OutputPath is supplied, emits JSON only when OutputPath is omitted, installs no dependencies, calls no network, reads no secrets, creates or mutates no external repositories, touches no production, billing, authentication, authorization, database, CI/CD security, deployment, MCP mutation, cleanup enforcement, issue #194, or digital twin implementation.
risk_result: Medium risk and contained. The change adds a CLI surface and artifact family, but it is bounded to repository-local specification evidence and validation coverage.
unresolved_risks: Claude Code provider execution was attempted but did not complete in this run; Codex completed the local implementation under the same contract.
The starter standard intentionally does not create repositories, install dependencies, deploy, connect wallets, configure billing, or implement external product integrations.
merge_status: PR #238 merged on 2026-06-22T14:26:24Z at merge commit 57e8fa822fb456f6de79196ea8a11f17c56778af. Issue #237 closed automatically as completed and post-merge closure evidence was recorded.
deployment_status: Not applicable. No deployment is allowed by this contract.
